summary: Bind thumbnail persistence evidence to the exact pre-exit and fresh-process pre-reopen boundary.

decisions.decision: Capture the unique persisted thumbnail PNG digest immediately before deleting the packaged WebDriver session, capture it again in the fresh application/WebView process before reopening the project, and require those digests to match exactly in both live and retained verification. Continue to compare canonical geometry identity after project reopen.
decisions.rationale: The earlier Files and Welcome screenshots can be followed by another valid same-geometry thumbnail write before shutdown. They are presentation evidence, not the process-persistence boundary. Exact pre-exit versus fresh-process pre-reopen bytes directly prove durable restart persistence and fail on any replacement at the boundary that matters.

testEvidence.criterion: Evidence must retain equal canonical digests from immediately before process exit and from the fresh process before reopen.
testEvidence.test: Require beforeCloseThumbnailSha256 throughout the walkthrough type, live validator, retained verifier, runner ordering contract, and tamper fixture.

red.outcome: RED: 4 failed, 72 passed because the implementation did not capture or validate the exact pre-exit digest.

green.outcome: GREEN: 76/76 tests passed with live and retained exact-boundary equality plus runner ordering coverage.

nearMisses.sought: Exact clean packaged native M4 restart persistence evidence
nearMisses.confirmation: The run passed release build, cube/STL, MCP, cache, geometry delta, animation, and thumbnail presentation, then showed that the current pre-reopen persisted PNG differed from the older Files-view PNG for the same geometry. Host cleanup passed.

questions: No owner decision is required; this moves the byte-equality oracle to the literal process-restart boundary.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
